patterns.name: Permission Guard + Content Split (Page / PageContent)
patterns.description: Every guarded feature page is split into two components: a thin `*Page` that handles permission loading and access-denied rendering, and a `*PageContent` that receives resolved permissions as props and renders the real UI. This pattern is used consistently in admin, payments, and studio apps.
patterns.verdict: Well-designed, should be documented as the standard pattern.

patterns.name: Permission loading renders null (invisible loading)
patterns.description: All *Page components that check permissions return `null` while `isLoading` is true, preventing flash of AccessDeniedState. Some do `return null;` and some `return null` without braces or semicolons — minor formatting inconsistency but consistent in intent.
examples: if (isLoading) return null;  // admin/settings, admin/users, etc.
if (isLoading) { return null; }  // payments/checkout, payments/orders, etc.
patterns.verdict: Consistent intent, minor formatting inconsistency (one-liner vs block). Could be standardised to one form.

patterns.name: Feature index.ts exports only the page component
patterns.description: Feature `index.ts` barrel files export only the minimal public surface — usually just the page component(s). This keeps cross-feature coupling minimal.

patterns.name: App-level route files are thin wrappers
patterns.description: Next.js `page.tsx` files in the `app/` directory do nothing but call `setRequestLocale` and render the feature page component. Business logic is zero.
patterns.verdict: Perfectly compliant with the architecture rule.

patterns.name: AccessDeniedState re-exported from shared package via local shim
patterns.description: Each app's `shared/presentation/components/AccessDeniedState.tsx` is a one-liner re-export from the `shared` package (`export { AccessDeniedState } from 'shared/components'`). This preserves the absolute import path `@/shared/presentation/components/AccessDeniedState` across apps while keeping the implementation in the shared package.

patterns.name: Duplicate hook files (application root vs application/hooks/)
patterns.description: Several features have two copies of the same hook: one at `application/*.ts` (an older version using `createBrowserSupabaseClient` + `useMemo`) and another at `application/hooks/*.ts` (the current version using `useSupabase` from `shared`). The `application/hooks/` version is what production code imports; the root-level files appear to be leftover migration artefacts.
patterns.recommendation: Delete the root-level legacy hook files. Verify no import points to them, then remove.

patterns.name: Duplicate component folders with different casing (PascalCase vs kebab-case)
patterns.description: Two feature folders contain identical files duplicated under two differently-named directories: `ProductDetail/` (PascalCase) and `product-detail/` (kebab-case) in the store, and `InlineEditor/` (PascalCase) and `inline-editor/` (kebab-case) in studio. `diff` confirms the files are identical. This wastes disk space, confuses navigation, and may cause import ambiguity.
patterns.recommendation: Keep only one version (kebab-case for folders, per naming conventions). Remove the PascalCase folder after verifying all imports point to the kept one.

patterns.name: Inconsistent Supabase client instantiation in hooks
patterns.description: Two approaches coexist: (1) `useSupabase()` from the `shared` package (current, preferred) and (2) `useMemo(() => createBrowserSupabaseClient(), [])` (older, raw). The legacy hooks in studio and store still use the raw `useMemo` form. The preferred hook form wraps the same logic but keeps instantiation consistent and testable.
examples: PREFERRED: import { useSupabase } from 'shared'; const supabase = useSupabase();
LEGACY: import { createBrowserSupabaseClient } from 'api/supabase'; const supabase = useMemo(() => createBrowserSupabaseClient(), []);
patterns.recommendation: Migrate all hooks to `useSupabase()`. `useDelegateSearch.ts` and `useCartFromCookie.ts` are in the `hooks/` layer but still use the old form — these should be updated.

patterns.name: AppTopNavigation implemented slightly differently across apps
patterns.description: All 7 apps have a local `AppTopNavigation.tsx` that wraps `@monorepo/app-components`'s `AppNavigation`, but they pass `permissionState` in four different ways: (1) spread via `useCurrentUserPermissions()` return directly, (2) destructure and create inline object, (3) destructure and assign to a `permissionState` variable first, (4) destructure with different prop ordering. All compile to the same result but the inconsistency suggests the component was copied and independently tweaked.
patterns.recommendation: Standardise to the simplest approach: `const permissionState = useCurrentUserPermissions(); return <AppNavigation {...props} permissionState={permissionState} />;`

patterns.name: Supabase tables not in generated types use `as any` pattern
patterns.description: Two infrastructure query files suppress TypeScript errors for table names not yet in the generated Supabase types using `const TABLE = '...' as any;` with an ESLint disable comment. This is a temporary workaround that should be resolved by regenerating Supabase types.
patterns.recommendation: Run `pnpm codegen:supabase` to regenerate types and remove the `as any` casts.

patterns.name: Magic staleTime value repeated without constants in some hooks
patterns.description: Most hooks correctly reference a named constant (e.g., `SETTINGS_STALE_TIME_MS`, `ORDERS_STALE_TIME_MS`) for their TanStack Query `staleTime`. However, several hooks inline the raw number directly (`staleTime: 60_000` or `staleTime: 30_000`) without naming it. The value 60_000 appears 10+ times inline; 30_000 appears 5 times inline.
patterns.recommendation: Move 60_000 and 30_000 to named constants in each feature's domain/constants.ts (or to a shared time constants file). This already exists in the `shared` package: `shared/constants/time` — extend it or add a `DEFAULT_STALE_TIME_MS` there.

patterns.name: Test mock pattern for useTranslations is consistent and idiomatic
patterns.description: All test files mock `useTranslations` the same way: `useTranslations: () => (key: string) => key`. This returns the translation key itself, making assertions like `toHaveTextContent('title')` stable and locale-independent.
patterns.verdict: Well-established pattern across all apps. Worth documenting in testing rules.

ruleSuggestions.title: Page/PageContent Split Pattern
ruleSuggestions.description: Every guarded page MUST be split into a thin `*Page` (permission check + access-denied) and a `*PageContent` (actual UI with resolved permissions passed as props). The `*Page` returns `null` while permissions load.
ruleSuggestions.rationale: Already used uniformly across admin, payments, and studio. Formalising it prevents inconsistent implementations in new features.

ruleSuggestions.title: Hooks must live in application/hooks/ only
ruleSuggestions.description: Hook files MUST be placed in `application/hooks/` and never directly under `application/`. Files at `application/*.ts` that are hooks are legacy artefacts and should be deleted after migration.
ruleSuggestions.rationale: Removes ambiguity about which version is canonical and prevents bugs from importing the wrong copy.
ruleSuggestions.appliesTo: All apps — especially studio and store

ruleSuggestions.title: Always use useSupabase() from shared — never raw createBrowserSupabaseClient in hooks
ruleSuggestions.description: Hooks that need a Supabase client MUST use `import { useSupabase } from 'shared'; const supabase = useSupabase();`. Direct calls to `createBrowserSupabaseClient()` inside hooks are forbidden.
ruleSuggestions.rationale: Standardises the instantiation pattern, simplifies mocking in tests, and ensures a single Supabase client singleton per render.

ruleSuggestions.title: Component group folders must use kebab-case
ruleSuggestions.description: Component group folders (non-single-component directories) must use kebab-case. PascalCase folders are reserved for future single-component folder groups only if the component name itself requires it — and even then kebab-case is preferred.
ruleSuggestions.rationale: Removes the ambiguity that led to duplicated PascalCase/kebab-case folder pairs in store and studio.

positiveObservations.observation: All 7 apps' globals.css files are byte-for-byte identical — CSS consistency rule is perfectly enforced.
nuqs is used correctly and consistently for URL state in all filterable/paginated pages (audit log, users, store products, studio products, received orders).
positiveObservations.files: Multiple SearchParams domain files and PageContent hooks

positiveObservations.observation: Domain constants (query keys, stale times, pagination sizes) are well-organised in feature domain/constants.ts files. The majority of hooks reference named constants rather than magic numbers.
Test coverage of `*Page` permission-guard components is thorough — each Page component has a corresponding `.test.tsx` that verifies loading, access-denied, and content-rendered states.
The `useTranslations: () => (key: string) => key` test mock is applied uniformly across all 90+ test files, ensuring locale-independent assertions.
